My neighbors (73-2 Baldwin Street Boston) have installed a wind chime in the public alley between our apartments. It's keeping the kids awake. They are upset with my landlord and I believe they are taking it out on his tenants (me and my family). The law states: "In Boston, noisy wind chimes violate city noise ordinances if they exceed 50 dBa between 11 p.m. and 7 a.m." These noise makers are registering much higher than that all throughout the night and day.

Concerns of the increased traffic that travels up Old Harbor St from East Eighth in the wrong direction. I believe that this stems from the contractors working on the projects decision to redesign the intersection and removed all the signs.
